scenario_scope: Real local-project intake decision for the pinned MoECOT Manifest compiler-era snapshot.

cases.decision_rationale: MoECOT supplies a compiler-first integration reference and cross-layer negative evidence. Its mechanisms belong in existing semantic compiler, provenance, routing, improvement, benchmark, and stewardship chapters.
required_before_drafting: keep numeric results source-reported unless reproduced
evidence_transition_preconditions: accept a claim-specific evidence transition before promotion
promotion_blockers: project experiments not reproduced by the book repository
non_claims: does not establish a deployed integrated ASI stack
does not establish MoECOT capability, safety, or corrigibility

cases.decision_rationale: Compilation-wide governance is important but currently functions as a cross-cutting pattern spanning existing compiler, artifact, supply-chain, and integrated-architecture chapters. A separate chapter would duplicate those owners unless later cross-project mining reveals an unowned boundary.
required_before_drafting: review whether compilation-wide governance can be expressed as a cross-chapter invariant
avoid duplicating the integrated reference architecture
evidence_transition_preconditions: identify a distinct chapter job not owned elsewhere
promotion_blockers: existing chapters already own the component mechanisms
project provenance is not yet deduplicated
non_claims: does not add the proposed chapter
does not claim the compiler-first pattern is novel or validated

validation_boundary: Records the reviewed MoECOT local-project intake and an explicitly deferred chapter split. It does not validate project results, source publication rights, or a support-state transition.
non_claims: does not publish private source material
